label: SaaS Application
description: Multi-tenant SaaS with auth, billing and a dashboard.

fields.label: Project name
fields.placeholder: Acme Analytics

fields.label: What does it do?
fields.placeholder: A dashboard that lets teams track and share product metrics.

fields.label: Extra constraints
fields.placeholder: Must deploy to Vercel; GDPR compliant; <200ms p95.

template:
You are a senior full-stack engineer. Build a production-grade SaaS application called "{{projectName}}".

## Product
{{summary}}

## Tech stack
- Platform: {{stack}}
- Language: {{language}}
{{#if frameworks}}- Frameworks/libraries: {{frameworks}}
{{/if}}- Database: {{database}}

## Required capabilities
{{#if auth}}- Authentication: email/password + OAuth, session management, password reset.
{{/if}}{{#if multitenant}}- Multi-tenancy: organizations/workspaces with role-based access (owner, admin, member).
{{/if}}{{#if billing}}- Billing: Stripe subscriptions with a free tier, plan upgrades/downgrades, and a billing portal.
{{/if}}- A dashboard as the primary authenticated surface.
- Clean, accessible, responsive UI.

## Engineering expectations
- Idiomatic, well-structured {{language}} with clear module boundaries.
- Input validation and explicit error handling on every entry point.
{{#if tests}}- Automated tests for core flows (unit + integration) and instructions to run them.
{{/if}}- A README covering setup, environment variables, and how to run locally.

{{#if constraints}}## Constraints
{{constraints}}

{{/if}}## Deliverables
1. A short plan of the architecture and data model before coding.
2. The complete, runnable codebase organized by feature.
3. Setup instructions and seed/sample data.

Ask clarifying questions only if a requirement is ambiguous; otherwise make sensible, well-documented decisions and proceed.

label: Website with Animations
description: Marketing / portfolio site with motion and polish.

fields.label: Site name
fields.placeholder: Nova Studio

fields.label: Purpose & sections
fields.placeholder: Agency site: hero, work showcase, services, about, contact.

fields.label: Visual vibe
fields.placeholder: Bold, editorial, dark mode, lots of whitespace

fields.label: Extra constraints
fields.placeholder: Lighthouse 95+; no heavy 3D; ships in one file per route.

template:
You are an award-winning frontend engineer and motion designer. Build a {{animationLevel}} animated website called "{{projectName}}".

## Brief
{{summary}}

## Look & feel
{{#if vibe}}Visual direction: {{vibe}}.
{{/if}}Deliver a cohesive design system (type scale, color, spacing) before building.

## Tech
- Platform: {{stack}}
{{#if frameworks}}- Styling/libraries: {{frameworks}}
{{/if}}- Use modern CSS and a lightweight animation approach; avoid janky, layout-thrashing animations.

## Motion direction
- Entrance animations on scroll, smooth section transitions, and meaningful micro-interactions.
{{#if animationLevel == "wow"}}- Push for a memorable signature moment (hero reveal or interactive centerpiece) without hurting performance.
{{/if}}{{#if a11y}}- Honor prefers-reduced-motion and keep all content accessible and keyboard-navigable.
{{/if}}- Target 60fps; prefer transform/opacity; lazy-load heavy assets.

{{#if constraints}}## Constraints
{{constraints}}

{{/if}}## Deliverables
1. The design system + a one-paragraph motion rationale.
2. The complete, runnable code, component by component.
3. Notes on performance budget and how it was met.

Make confident design decisions and explain the key ones briefly.

label: Single-Page App
description: Client-rendered app around a focused workflow.

fields.label: App name
fields.placeholder: Kanbanly

fields.label: Core workflow
fields.placeholder: A drag-and-drop kanban board with boards, lists and cards.

template:
You are a senior frontend engineer. Build a single-page application called "{{projectName}}".

## Core workflow
{{summary}}

## Tech
- Language: {{language}}
{{#if frameworks}}- Frameworks/libraries: {{frameworks}}
{{/if}}- State management: {{state}} approach.
- Persistence: {{#if persistence == "local"}}offline-first using LocalStorage; data must survive reload.{{/if}}{{#if persistence == "api"}}a typed backend API client with optimistic updates.{{/if}}{{#if persistence == "supabase"}}Supabase (auth + database) with realtime where it helps.{{/if}}

## Expectations
- A clear component architecture and data flow described up front.
- Responsive, accessible UI with sensible empty/loading/error states.
- No unnecessary dependencies.
{{#if tests}}- Tests for the core workflow and instructions to run them.
{{/if}}
## Deliverables
1. Architecture sketch + state shape.
2. Complete runnable code.
3. README with setup and run steps.

Proceed with well-reasoned defaults; only ask if truly blocked.

label: REST / JSON API

fields.label: Service name
fields.placeholder: orders-api

fields.label: What it manages
fields.placeholder: CRUD for orders and line items, with status transitions.

fields.label: Non-functional requirements
fields.placeholder: Rate limiting, pagination, idempotency keys, <100ms p95.

label: CLI Tool

fields.label: Command name
fields.placeholder: snapsync

fields.label: What it does
fields.placeholder: Sync a local folder to S3 with diffing and a dry-run mode.

template:
You are an expert in building ergonomic command-line tools. Build a CLI called "{{projectName}}".

## Purpose
{{summary}}

## Tech
- Language: {{language}}
- Distribution: {{distribution}}.

## UX requirements
- Clear `--help`, sensible subcommands, and good defaults.
- Helpful, non-cryptic error messages and correct exit codes.
- A `--dry-run` / verbose option where it makes sense.
{{#if interactive}}- Interactive prompts when required arguments are missing (but always scriptable via flags).
{{/if}}{{#if tests}}- Automated tests for argument parsing and core behavior.
{{/if}}
## Deliverables
1. Command/flag design before coding.
2. Complete runnable code.
3. README with install and usage examples.

Favor a small dependency footprint and explain key design choices briefly.

label: Analytics Dashboard
description: A data-dense dashboard with charts, filters and drill-downs.

fields.label: Dashboard name
fields.placeholder: Revenue Cockpit

fields.label: What does it monitor?
fields.placeholder: Live sales KPIs, funnel conversion and per-region breakdowns.

fields.label: Key metrics & widgets
fields.placeholder: MRR, churn, active users, top accounts table, trend lines.

fields.label: Extra constraints
fields.placeholder: Sub-second loads; role-scoped data; exportable to CSV.

template:
You are a senior frontend engineer specializing in data UIs. Build an analytics dashboard called "{{projectName}}".

## Purpose
{{summary}}

## Tech
- Platform: {{stack}}
{{#if frameworks}}- Libraries: {{frameworks}}
{{/if}}- Data source: {{database}}

## Metrics & widgets
{{metrics}}

## Requirements
- A clear information hierarchy: headline KPIs first, supporting detail below.
- Honest, accessible charts with units, empty/loading/error states.
{{#if filters}}- Global filters (date range, segment) that update every widget consistently.
{{/if}}{{#if realtime}}- Realtime or near-realtime data with smooth, non-jarring updates.
{{/if}}- Responsive layout that stays readable on smaller screens.

{{#if constraints}}## Constraints
{{constraints}}

{{/if}}## Deliverables
1. Layout wireframe and the metric/query list before coding.
2. The complete, runnable code organized by widget.
3. README with setup, data wiring, and run steps.

Make well-reasoned defaults and explain key trade-offs briefly.

fields.label: What does it do?

fields.label: Permissions needed

label: Realtime Collaboration App
description: Multi-user app with live sync, presence and conflict handling.

fields.label: App name
fields.placeholder: CoSketch

fields.label: What do users collaborate on?
fields.placeholder: A shared whiteboard where teams draw and comment together live.

template:
You are a senior engineer specializing in realtime systems. Build a realtime collaboration app called "{{projectName}}".

## What users collaborate on
{{summary}}

## Tech
- Platform: {{stack}}
- Language: {{language}}
- Database: {{database}}
- Transport: {{transport}}

## Realtime requirements
- Conflict resolution: {{conflict}} — explain how concurrent edits converge.
- Optimistic local updates with reconciliation on server confirmation.
- Reconnect gracefully and resync state after disconnects.
{{#if presence}}- Live presence: who's online and live cursors/selections.
{{/if}}- Handle late joiners receiving the current state correctly.
{{#if tests}}- Tests covering concurrent-edit and reconnect scenarios.
{{/if}}
## Deliverables
1. Sync architecture and data model before coding.
2. The complete, runnable codebase (client + server).
3. README: setup, env vars, and how to test multi-user locally.

Make production-grade defaults and explain the consistency trade-offs.

label: Authentication System
description: Secure auth: sessions, MFA, password reset and RBAC.

fields.label: System / service name
fields.placeholder: acme-auth

fields.label: Context & who logs in
fields.placeholder: B2B app: employees log in, admins manage org members.

label: Microservice
description: A single-responsibility service built to run in production.

fields.label: Service name
fields.placeholder: notification-service

fields.label: Single responsibility
fields.placeholder: Owns sending and tracking transactional emails and push notifications.

template:
You are a senior backend engineer experienced with distributed systems. Build a microservice called "{{serviceName}}".

## Responsibility
{{responsibility}}
Keep the scope tight — this service owns one thing and exposes a clear contract.

## Tech
- Language: {{language}}
{{#if frameworks}}- Framework: {{frameworks}}
{{/if}}- Datastore: {{database}}
- Inbound interface: {{comms}}

## Requirements
- A versioned, documented service contract and explicit error model.
- Health/readiness endpoints, graceful shutdown, and config via environment.
- Idempotent handlers and retry-safe behavior for external calls.
{{#if messaging}}- Async messaging: publish/consume with at-least-once handling and a dead-letter path.
{{/if}}{{#if observability}}- Observability: structured logs, key metrics, and request tracing with correlation IDs.
{{/if}}{{#if tests}}- Automated tests (unit + contract/integration) and instructions to run them.
{{/if}}
## Deliverables
1. Service contract, data model, and dependency list before coding.
2. The complete, runnable service with a Dockerfile.
3. README: setup, env vars, run, and local end-to-end test steps.

Use production-grade defaults and explain notable trade-offs briefly.
